cast: Ahmed Rizq, Ayten Amer, Dalia Mostafa, Nedal Al Shafeay, Salah Abdallah, Hosny Sheta, Mohamed Mahran, Walaa ElSherif

title: Sukkar's Crisis
overview: Sukkar, a rural man, suddenly inherits a large fortune from his wealthy uncle who used to live in the United States. As Sukkar discovers that he has to share the inheritance with his cousins, comic situations unfold as Sukkar and his American-raised cousins try to adjust to one another.
cast: Ahmed Eid, Hanan Motawie, Feriyal Yousif, Rahma Hassan, Sameh El-Sereety, Lotfy Labib, Said Tarabeek, Ahlam Al Gritly

title: Lost village
overview: Tucked away in the Syrian mountains, the fictional village of Umm al-Tanafs al-Fawqa has been completely bypassed by modern technology — and its simple, warm-hearted residents have no idea what they're missing. Each episode follows their hilarious attempts to navigate life's big and small dramas with nothing but common sense, stubbornness and each other. A gentle, sharp satire that uses village innocence as a mirror for modern society's complications — described by many viewers as the greatest Arab comedy ever made.
cast: Bassem Yakhour, Nedal Sejari, Tolay Haroon, Zuhair Ramadan, Ghada Bashour, Rawad Alio, Jirjes Jbarah, Jamal El-Ali

title: Arabesque: The Days of Hassan Al-Noaamany
overview: The series follows the life of Hassan, an old chivalrous Arabesque workshop owner who lives in a poor neighborhood with his family. The series provides an insight into the day-to-day struggles of the family as Hassan gets involved in much of their neighbors' disputes and affairs.
cast: Salah El-Saadany, Huda Sultan, Abu Bakr Ezzat, Hala Sedki, Hesham Selim, Karam Metawea, Lucy, Hassan Hosny

title: Beit Baba
overview: A retired principal comes home to his “model” family—only to learn the apple didn’t just fall far from the tree, it rolled into chaos. Secrets spill, halos slip, and fixing the mess proves harder than running a school.
cast: Mohamed Anwar, Entesar, Sami Maghouri, Mariam El Gendy, Mohamed Mahmoud, Ahmed Abdelhamid, Selim Hani, Rania Mahmoud Yasseen
tagline: At my dad's house... every day is a problem, and every problem has a laugh

title: Qasr El Nil
overview: Dina El-Sherbiny's husband dies, and circumstances force her to marry her husband's brother, and disputes arise between her and his first wife.
cast: Dina El Sherbiny, Sabry Fawaz, Reham Abdelghafour, Ahmed Khalid Saleh, Salah Abdallah, Ahmad Magdy, Mariam El Khosht, Omar El Saeed

title: My Way
overview: An aspiring artist tries to make it, facing numerous difficulties both personally and professionally throughout her life, since her persecuted childhood and adolescence as a girl living in a rural area.
cast: Sherine Abdel Wahab, Ahmed Fahmy, Bassel Khayyat, Sawsan Badr, Mahmoud El Gendy, Laila Ezz El Arab, Mohamed Mamdouh, Mohamed Adel

title: Laysa Kull Ma Tara Huwa Ma Yabdu
overview: It includes seven separate stories, each story will be presented in five episodes.
tagline: Sometimes, illusion is better than reality.

title: Bwabet Elhalwani
overview: Bwabet El‑Halawani brings to life the human stories behind Egypt’s transformation during the Suez Canal era. Through craftsmen, families, and dreamers caught in political and social upheaval, the series blends history with personal struggle, showing how ambition, sacrifice, and resilience shaped a defining moment in Egypt’s past.
cast: Samira Abdulaziz, Ahmad Rateb, Nashwa Mustafa, Khaled El Nabawy, Hassan Hosny, Mimi Gamal, Mohamed Wafiq, Hassan Kamy

title: Gouging The Sea
overview: The series is set in Upper Egypt and is based on a true event in which the tyrannical parents of Abdul Hayy, a rebellious young man, force him to face several difficulties
cast: Mohammad Reyad, Kamal Abo Raya, Mona Abd El Ghani, Samah Alsaeed, Medhat Tekha, Mofid Ashour, Radwa Ehab, Salah Abdallah

title: The Oracle
overview: A crook manages to evade capture for a long time as he switches between various identities that make it difficult for the police to catch him. As he is finally caught by the police officer who has spent his life chasing him, both of them are changed forever by a series of events.
cast: Adel Emam, Hussein Fahmy, Said Tarabeek, Ahmed Falawkas, Nehal Anbar, Mohamed ElShaqanqeery, Sherif Ramzy, Sherine

title: Reeh El-Madam
overview: A comic TV series in which Sultan is a commercials director who is addicted to multiple secret relationships which seems to always get him in trouble. He is also challenged by his wife, Dalia's short-term memory loss as she forgets who he is every day. Together with his friend, Bahgat, they set daily plans in motion so he could win his wife's heart and face all his other dilemmas.
cast: Ahmad Fahmy, Akram Hosni, Mai Omar, Ragaa Al-Giddawy, Ali Hemeida, El Khalil Comedy, Mai Fakhry, Islam Ibrahim

title: Question Mark
overview: Noah Al-Shawwaf appears to be a well-off gentleman from Alexandria, but he is running away from a mysterious past.
cast: Mohamed Ragab, Haitham Ahmed Zaki, Mirhan Hussein, Mahmoud El-Bezzawy, Gehan Khalil, Abdul Rahman Abu Zahra, Hassan El-Adl, Emad Rashad

title: Princess Beesa
overview: Beesa, who lives with her grandmother Siksika, works in the local wedding industry. She inherits a school from an uncle, and pursues her dream of opening her own wedding venue named "Princess Beesa".
cast: Mai Ezz ElDein, Amir El-Masry, Mohamed Anwar, Poussi, Hagag Abdel Azim, Eman Al Sayyed, Hossam Dagher, Sami Maghouri

title: His All-Knowing Secret
overview: Between the past and the present, the events of the series follow Sultan Hamed, a popular hero at the time of the French campaign who turns into a legend that has shrines in many villages of Egypt. At the same time, Hamed, a jewelry maker, devotes his life to revealing the sultan's secret and finds himself fleeing from antiquity smugglers.
cast: Ahmad Fahmy, Ahmed Salah ElSaadany, Hussein Fahmy, Hanan Motawie, Reem Mostafa, Rania Toumi, Shams, Ahmed Abdelaziz

title: Alseera AlAshouria: AlHarafeesh
overview: Based on the novel by Naguib Mahfouz (The Harafish), the series presents the biography of the popular hero Ashour Al-Naji and his family, as he represents a symbol of principles, values, justice, mercy and fairness to the simple.
cast: Yasmine Gamal, Sami Maghouri, Raouf Moustafa, Ashraf Tulba, Marwa ElKhatib, عصام نجاتي, Rogena, Kamal Abo Raya

title: Eshref Ruya
overview: As a child, Eşref fell in love from afar with a girl he called “Rüya” and spent years searching for her, only to become a powerful mafia figure along the way. Meanwhile, Nisan, an idealistic musician, finds herself in grave danger after performing at a wedding held at Eşref’s hotel. Eşref falls for Nisan, unaware that she is both the long-lost Rüya he has been chasing for years and a police informant. Trapped in a whirlwind of love, betrayal, and power struggles, Eşref is forced into a brutal reckoning—both with his criminal empire and his own emotions.
cast: Çağatay Ulusoy, Demet Özdemir, Büşra Develi, Necip Memili, Ahmet Rıfat Şungar, Tolga Tekin, Görkem Sevindik, Ceren Benderlioğlu

title: Kuruluş Osman
overview: The epic continuation of Diriliş Ertuğrul, Kuruluş Osman follows Osman Bey — son of Ertuğrul and destined founder of the Ottoman Empire — as he transforms a small Turkic tribe into a sovereign state. Battling the Byzantine Empire, Mongol forces and treachery from within, Osman must prove himself as both a warrior and a leader. A sweeping historical action drama built on faith, honour and the birth of one of history's greatest empires.
cast: Burak Özçivit, Özge Törer, Emre Bey, Ecem Sena Bayır, Ömer Faruk Aran, Belgin Şimşek, Leya Kırşan, Yiğit Uçan

title: The One I Love
overview: Erkan Aldur, a young man from a wealthy Istanbul family, is stationed in eastern Turkey to complete his mandatory military service in a remote, earthquake-stricken region. In this harsh and unfamiliar landscape, he meets Dicle, a young woman who has lived her entire life in the village and whose world is shaped by tradition, family, and community. Their encounter brings together two very different worlds, setting in motion a journey of change, resilience, and unexpected connection. As Dicle steps into the unfamiliar and Erkan faces new responsibilities, their story unfolds into a heartfelt and transformative tale of love and courage, that bridges even the widest divides
cast: Aytaç Şaşmaz, Helin Kandemir, Hüseyin Avni Danyal, Esra Ronabar, Deniz Işın, Cihat Süvarioğlu, Özlem Conker, Barış Baktaş

title: Golden Boy
overview: When Istanbul playboy Ferit Korhan's exasperated grandfather arranges his marriage to a girl from Gaziantep, fate intervenes — Ferit falls for Seyran, the younger sister of his intended bride, upending both families. Starring Afra Saraçoğlu and Mert Ramazan Demir, this record-breaking Turkish drama weaves together a forced marriage, dark family secrets and a love story built on constant conflict, sold to over 130 territories worldwide.
cast: Afra Saraçoğlu, Mert Ramazan Demir, Çetin Tekindor, Şerif Sezer, Gülçin Santırcıoğlu, Emre Altuğ, Gözde Kansu, Beril Pozam

title: Deep In Love
overview: In a land where people are accustomed to battling fierce waves and constant storms, the story of a woman who stands tall on her feet, with all her emotional strength, resilience and endurance, and a man who is passionate about his "love" and who competes with the storms of the Black Sea and struggles with its waves, comes to the screens with an effective script... The ups and downs of the mountains, the sea and the human soul are told through the eyes of a stranger who finds himself in the middle of the endless war between two mortal enemies.
cast: Ulaş Tuna Astepe, Deniz Baysal, Ava Yaman, Burak Yörük, Yeşim Ceren Bozoğlu, Onur Dilber, Erdem Şanlı, Burcu Cavrar

title: Yeralti
overview: Haydar Ali avenges his family's murder and ends up in prison, where he falls into the orbit of Turkey's most dangerous crime cartel. Recruited by intelligence to dismantle it from within, he must earn the trust of its ruthless boss — a man who becomes his closest ally. Three years later, freedom brings the deadliest complication yet: the woman he never stopped loving is now that same man's wife. Dark, cinematic and morally complex — Turkey's answer to Peaky Blinders.
cast: Deniz Can Aktaş, Uraz Kaygılaroğlu, Devrim Özkan, Sümeyye Aydoğan, Burak Sevinç, Ülkü Hilal Çiftçi, Koray Şahinbaş, Hülya Gülşen Irmak

title: Forbidden Fruit
overview: Two sisters with contrasting values are drawn into Istanbul's glamorous and treacherous high society. Yıldız craves wealth and status, while principled Zeynep falls for her cold, powerful boss Alihan. When Yıldız becomes entangled with married tycoon Halit and his scheming wife Ender, a web of desire, manipulation and betrayal unfolds across Istanbul's elite world. A addictive soap opera that ran for six seasons and 177 episodes, driven by sharp dialogue and constantly escalating drama.
cast: Şevval Sam, Eda Ece, Sevda Erginci, Barış Aytaç, Vildan Vatansever, Bekir Aksoy, Biran Damla Yılmaz, Murat Aygen

title: The Shadow Team
overview: A team of elite agents within Turkey's National Intelligence Organisation are recruited for covert operations — on the condition that the outside world believes they are dead. Led by veteran officer Mete Bey, the shadow team carries out high-stakes missions at home and abroad, battling terrorist networks and foreign threats to national security. Drawn from real events, it's Turkey's answer to the spy thriller genre — patriotic, action-packed and relentlessly tense.
cast: Tolga Sarıtaş, Rabia Soytürk, Yunus Emre Yıldırımer, Serdar Yeğin, Melisa Akman, Yakup Aksel

title: Mehmed: Sultan of Conquests
overview: The epic story of Mehmed II — the young Ottoman prince who defied palace enemies, survived treachery and deposition, and returned to fulfil his life's dream: the conquest of Constantinople. Spanning his rise from a doubted heir to one of history's most consequential rulers, the series unfolds across palace intrigue, battlefield warfare and a psychological chess match between two great empires. Praised as the finest Turkish historical series in years — cinematic, fast-paced and historically grounded.
cast: Serkan Çayoğlu, Sinan Albayrak, Bülent Alkış, Ertuğrul Postoğlu, Ali Sinan Demir, Recep Çavdar, Kenan Çoban, Sena Çakır

title: Far Away
overview: Alya Albora arrives in Mardin with her late husband’s funeral and their five-year-old son to fulfill her husband’s last will. However, this arrival marks a point of no return, and there is no way out of Albora. The head of the Albora tribe, Cihan Albora, is not indifferent to Alya’s struggles, but he refuses to let her take her child and leave. Confronting the darkness of the past, hidden secrets, and the harsh realities of the region, Alya Albora finds herself caught in a fierce struggle with her late husband’s family.
cast: Ozan Akbaba, Sinem Ünsal, Gonca Cilasun, Müfit Kayacan, Alper Çankaya, Ferit Kaya, Atakan Özkaya, Sahra Şaş

title: Boundless Love
overview: When he was a little child, Halil İbrahim lost his father due to a blood feud and was exiled to Istanbul. Twenty years later, he returns to his homeland in the Karadeniz region as a handsome, powerful young man. He plans to marry the girl he loves, Yasemin, and start a new life. However, events do not allow this. Halil İbrahim embarks on a journey of revenge, and his life will change completely when he encounters Zeynep from the Leto family.
cast: Deniz Can Aktaş, Miray Daner, Mehmet Korhan Fırat, Naz Çağla Irmak, Burak Sevinç, Cansın Şenel, Emre Bulut, Yaren Yapici

title: The Pit
overview: When a mafia family named Koçovars are in danger of losing control of their neighborhood, “The Pit,” their youngest son must come back home, a place he could never truly escape.
cast: Aras Bulut Iynemli, Erkan Kolçak Köstendil, Necip Memili, Kadir Çermik, Perihan Savaş, Mustafa Kirantepe, Rıza Kocaoğlu, Burak Dakak

title: Founder: Orhan
overview: Kuruluş: Orhan is a Turkish historical drama that follows the life of Orhan Bey, the son of Osman I and the second ruler of the Ottoman Empire. Set in the 14th century, the series portrays Orhan’s transformation from a young warrior into a visionary leader who expands his father's legacy by conquering key Byzantine cities like Bursa and establishing the foundations of a powerful empire. With political intrigue, epic battles, and complex relationships, the show explores Orhan’s rise amidst internal strife and external threats, continuing the legacy of Kuruluş: Osman with a new generation of characters and high-stakes storytelling.
cast: Mert Yazıcıoğlu, Mahassine Merabet, Alina Boz, Barış Falay, Burak Sergen, Bennu Yıldırımlar, Şükrü Özyıldız, İpek Arkan
tagline: The Next Era Begins

title: The Promise
overview: Reyhan is a sweet, small-town girl who agrees to marry a wealthy Istanbul playboy named Emir — as a favour to his dying father. Emir's plan? Make her life difficult enough that she'll walk away. What he didn't plan for was actually falling for her. A classic enemies-to-lovers slow burn wrapped in family drama, scheming in-laws and enough plot twists to keep you binge-watching well past bedtime — across hundreds of addictive episodes.
cast: Gökberk Demirci, Can Verel, Esra Çoban, Setenay Süer, Gül Arcan, Yagmur Sahbazova, Derya Kurtuluş Oktar, Gozde Gunduzlu

title: Sins and Roses
overview: As a boy, Serhat lost both his father and fortune but built an empire on family and integrity. His world shatters when his wife Berrak hides a devastating secret and falls into a coma. While searching for answers, his heart hardens—until he meets Zeynep, a passionate florist chasing the same truth, igniting a journey of trust, passion, and redemption.
cast: Murat Yildirim, Cemre Baysel, Serdar Orçin, Emel Çölgeçen, Oya Unustası, Ahmet Saraçoğlu, Aleyna Solaker, Serdar Özer

title: Love Is in the Air
overview: Eda, who ties all her hopes to her education, confronts Serkan Bolat, who cuts off her international education scholarship and leaves her with high school diploma. Serkan Bolat offers Eda to give her scholarship back if she pretends to be his fiance for two months. Although Eda rejects the offer of this man as she hates him, she has to accept it when the conditions change. While pretending to be engaged, Serkan and Eda begin to experience a passionate, challenging relationship that will make them forget all they know right. Because love is difficult. And that's why it's amazing.
cast: Hande Erçel, Kerem Bürsin, Evrim Doğan, Anıl İlter, Elçin Afacan, Başak Gümülcinelioğlu, Alican Aytekin, Sarp Bozkurt

title: Mahsun J
overview: Mahsun is a motorcycle courier in Istanbul. While searching for a way out of his life filled with debt, he accidentally discovers his "greatest" talent and decides to become a GIGOLO. Her manager will be her friend Leyla, who is a failed advertising executive.
cast: Mahsun Karaca, Eda Akalın

title: Family Secrets
overview: Ceylin is a fierce, rule-bending defence attorney whose father was wrongly imprisoned — for her, justice always comes before the law. Ilgaz is a by-the-book prosecutor who believes the two are the same thing. When his brother is accused of murder, he has no choice but to go to her for help. What follows is a gripping cat-and-mouse legal thriller built on brilliant opposing chemistry — winner of the International Emmy for Best Telenovela in 2023.
cast: Kaan Urgancıoğlu, Pınar Deniz, Uğur Polat, Zeyno Eracar, Uğur Aslan, Arda Anarat, Zeynep Atılgan, Pınar Çağlar Gençtürk

title: Cranberry Sorbet
overview: When free-spirited Doğa falls head over heels for charming Fatih, nobody expects the chaos that follows. His family is deeply conservative and devout; hers is modern and secular — and neither side is ready to back down. What starts as a love story quickly becomes a collision of two very different Turkeys, played out through family dinners, in-law battles and everything in between. Sharp, relatable and impossible to stop watching.
cast: Evrim Alasya, Barış Kılıç, Ahmet Mümtaz Taylan, Emre Dinler, Ceren Yalazoğlu Karakoç, Aliye Uzunatağan, Feyza Civelek, Selin Türkmen

title: Elif
overview: A kind 6-year-old girl is placed under the protection of her mother's best friend, who keeps a well-hidden secret, and is a maid for a wealthy family, in whose mansion she lives.
cast: Isabella Damla Güvenilir, Selin Sezgin, Deniz Irem Morkoç, Hasan Balliktas, Derya Şen, Kıvılcım Kaya, Melisa Döngel

title: The Valley of The Wolves
overview: One of Turkey's most iconic and influential dramas, Valley of the Wolves follows Polat Alemdar, an undercover agent who infiltrates Istanbul's most dangerous crime organisation after years of preparation. Combining organised crime, covert operations and sharp political commentary, the series fearlessly tackles Turkey's shadowy relationship between the state, the mafia and power — and became a cultural phenomenon that spawned multiple films and spin-offs.
genres: Action & Adventure, Drama, War & Politics, Crime
cast: Necati Şaşmaz, Özgü Namal, Kenan Çoban, Gürkan Uygun, İpek Tenolcay, Erhan Ufak, Emin Olcay, Serpil Tamur

title: Blood Flowers
overview: Dilan gave up all her dreams to marry Baran — a man from the family her family has been feuding with for years — to save her brother's life. Baran agreed to the marriage to stop the bloodshed, not to find love. What begins as a cold arrangement slowly becomes something neither of them planned for, while enemies circle from within and outside the family. A slow-burn daily romance built on blood feuds, reluctant hearts and glances that say more than words.
cast: Barış Baktaş, Yağmur Yüksel, Ekrem Aral Tuna, Dilek Güler, Nuray Şerefoğlu, Hasan Balliktas, Hilal Kuvvet, Yilmaz Ulutas

title: Hareem El-Sultan
overview: At the age of 26, when his reign began, Sultan Süleyman sought to build an empire more powerful than Alexander the Great and to render the Ottomans invincible. Throughout his 46-year reign, his fame as the greatest warrior and ruler of his age spreads both to the East and West.  With his companion Pargalı İbrahim, Süleyman achieves great victories, and makes his name known in the Muslim World. Süleyman called İbrahim his brother, friend and advisor. The television series shows Süleyman's consolidation of his power: Pargalı İbrahim being grand minister of state, reinforcing the rule of law throughout the empire, meeting foreign diplomats, and preparing for military campaigns, all set against the backdrop of the tension between Christian Europe and the Ottoman Empire.
cast: Halit Ergenç, Nur Fettahoğlu, Meryem Uzerli, Engin Öztürk, Merve Boluğur, Nebahat Çehre, Okan Yalabık, Selma Ergeç

title: 6 of Us
overview: A story of six siblings—Azize, Cemo, Zeliha, Fidan, Samet, and Balım—who, after suddenly losing their parents, struggle to stay united and survive. The roots of their tragedy trace back to their parents, Bala and Rıfat, whose forbidden love began in a village in Mardin. Rıfat, a teacher, falls in love with Bala, but Yavuz, the wealthy and obsessive son of Mardin’s richest family, kidnaps her. Despite his abuse, Bala escapes with Rıfat, and they build a secluded life in the forest, raising six children in peace. However, Yavuz’s revenge shatters their world when he finds and attacks them. Fleeing to Istanbul, the siblings face the dangers of the city while seeking refuge with “Uncle Aslan,” their father’s trusted friend. The story highlights resilience, as the older siblings are forced to grow up quickly to protect their younger brothers and sisters in the face of overwhelming challenges.
cast: Hazal Subaşı, Kaan Mirac Sezen, Turgut Tunçalp, Enes Koçak

title: Leyla
overview: As a child, Leyla suffers a blow from her stepmother and sees her world turned upside down. She loses everything - except the thirst for revenge. In search of justice, Leyla transforms into someone else and returns determined to settle accounts with the woman who destroyed her life and left her in a garbage dump. However, her revenge falters when she discovers the relationship between her ex-stepmother and the man, with whom she shared an innocent and unforgettable love affair
cast: Cemre Baysel, Gonca Vuslateri, Alperen Duymaz, Yiğit Kirazcı, Halil İbrahim Ceyhan, Melisa Duru Ünal, Gamze Karaduman, Dilara Aksüyek

title: Bahar
overview: Bahar gave up her medical career to be a devoted wife and mother — and got two decades of being taken for granted in return. When a sudden illness brings her face to face with death, she discovers just how little her "perfect" family values her. Surviving against the odds and finally opening her eyes, Bahar sets out to reclaim the life she sacrificed — returning to medicine, confronting her husband's secrets and rediscovering herself. A warm, funny and deeply satisfying story of a woman finally blooming.
cast: Demet Evgar, Buğra Gülsoy, Ecem Özkaya, Elit Andaç Çam, Demirhan Demircioğlu, Nil Sude Albayrak, Füsun Demirel, Hatice Aslan

title: My Siblings
overview: Kardeşlerim follows four orphaned siblings struggling to survive after losing their parents. Thrown into poverty and hardship, they rely on each other while navigating school, work and dangerous social divides in Istanbul. As they face betrayal, love and injustice, powerful enemies repeatedly threaten their fragile stability. Blending emotional family drama with romance and conflict, the series explores loyalty, sacrifice and resilience in the face of constant adversity.
cast: Su Burcu Yazgı Coşkun, Bilal Yiğit Koçak, Melis Minkari, Recep Usta, Fadik Sevin Atasoy, Cüneyt Mete, Celil Nalçakan, Ahu Yağtu

title: Daydreamer
overview: Daydreamer / Erkenci Kuş (2018) is a Turkish Star TV romantic comedy (51 episodes) starring Can Yaman and Demet Özdemir. Sanem, a cheerful aspiring writer working at her father's grocery shop, lands a job at Istanbul's top advertising agency to avoid an arranged marriage — and falls for Can, a globe-trotting photographer who returns to save his family's struggling business. Corporate rivalry, family secrets and irresistible chemistry follow. A global phenomenon, drawing millions of viewers across Italy, the Arab world and beyond.
cast: Demet Özdemir, Can Yaman, Birand Tunca, Öznur Serçeler, Ceren Taşçı, Sevcan Yaşar, Anıl Çelik, Tuan Tunalı

title: Tehran
overview: Tamar is a Mossad hacker-agent who infiltrates Tehran under a false identity to help destroy Iran's nuclear reactor. But when her mission fails, Tamar must plan an operation that will place everyone dear to her in jeopardy.
cast: Niv Sultan, Shaun Toub, Shervin Alenabi, Hugh Laurie
tagline: A secret agent embarks on her most dangerous mission yet.

title: Halef
overview: A successful Istanbul surgeon who escaped his traditional Urfa roots is forced to return home when his family demands he assume leadership as agha and marry Yildiz to end a decades-long feud. Already secretly married to Melek, he becomes trapped between two women, two worlds, and a web of family obligations. As buried secrets surface, love, duty, and revenge collide inside a deeply divided and tradition-bound family.
cast: İlhan Şen, Biran Damla Yılmaz, Onur Bilge, Veda Yurtsever, Aybüke Pusat, Sezin Bozacı, Mazlum Çimen, Mert Dogan

title: The Protector
overview: The epic adventure of Hakan, a young shopkeeper whose modern world gets turned upside down when he learns he’s connected to a secret, ancient order, tasked with protecting Istanbul.
cast: Çağatay Ulusoy, Hazar Ergüçlü, Okan Yalabık, Funda Eryiğit, Boran Kuzum

title: Bride of Istanbul
overview: Faruk is the owner of a bus company and the leader of a powerful family in Bursa which is a metropol in Turkey. Faruk meets a violonist girl and falls in love with her. But the dominant mother of Faruk isn't happy with her son's relationship.
cast: Aslı Enver, Özcan Deniz, Salih Bademci, Dilara Aksüyek, İpek Bilgin, Fırat Tanış, Neslihan Yeldan, Tamer Levent

title: Fauda
overview: A top Israeli agent comes out of retirement to hunt for a Palestinian militant he thought he'd killed, setting a chaotic chain of events into motion.
cast: Lior Raz, Netta Garti, Doron Ben-David, Rona-Lee Shimon, Itzik Cohen, Yaakov Zada Daniel

From the very first episode,

title: Endless Love
overview: Two separate worlds lie on two sides of the road in a seaside town. Both too close and way too far from each other. What happens when two young people from these worlds fall in love?  Kemal is one of the three children of a moderate family that lives in one of the inner neighborhoods of the town. His hand to mouth world has pinned all hopes on him. The motive of this world is not high aims, it’s to make a living and survive. It’s Kemal’s last year in mine engineering. His dreams are restricted with the truth and he doesn’t believe in miracles. Especially in miracles like love.  Kemal is soon tested with his disbelief and Nihan hops into his monotonous life. After that day Kemal’s world turns upside down and he loses control.
cast: Burak Özçivit, Neslihan Atagül, Kaan Urgancıoğlu, Zerrin Tekindor, Neşe Baykent, Hazal Filiz Küçükköse, Melisa Aslı Pamuk, Burak Sergen

title: Winds of Love
overview: Halil lost everything as a child when the powerful Aslanlı family destroyed his family — and he has spent years waiting for the right moment for revenge. Zeynep is that family's beloved, principled daughter, the last person he expected to fall for. When their worlds collide, Halil finds himself torn between a burning grudge and an unstoppable connection. A sweeping revenge romance in the tradition of Blood Flowers, sold to over 40 territories worldwide.
cast: Gökberk Yıldırım, Cemre Arda, Selma Kutluğ, Zeynep Serpil Bozkurt, Sude Oduncu, Serdar Yusuf, Dilek Aba, Adem Bal

title: Falling Leaves
overview: Yaprak Dökümü is an award-winning Turkish television series based on the novel of the same name by Reşat Nuri Güntekin. The series premiered on 13 September 2005 on Kanal D, and endеd its fifth and final season on 29 December 2010, comprising 174 episodes overall. The show is set in Istanbul, Turkey, and it revolves around the fictional family Tekin, which their arrival in Istanbul tears their family apart, and transforms them in a way they can't imagine.
cast: Halil Ergün, Güven Hokna, Bennu Yıldırımlar, Deniz Çakır, Tolga Karel, Gökçe Bahadır, Fahriye Evcen, Hasan Küçükçetin

title: Legacy
overview: The story of Yaman, a successful businessman who is incapable of love because of his traumatic childhood. There is only one person that he feels something for, his young nephew Yusuf. When Yusuf's mother dies unexpectedly, his aunt Seher, a beautiful, courageous young woman, moves into the family mansion to take care of him.
cast: Halil İbrahim Ceyhan, Berat Rüzgar Özkan, Binnaz Ekren, Ömer Gecü, Adnan Mehmet Ali Aslan, Oğuz Yağcı, Volkan Yildirim, Özge Agyar

title: Arafta
overview: Ateş, a man haunted by childhood scars and consumed by rage, returns to destroy the family that shattered his life years ago. His weapon is not a sword, but a marriage: 187 days with Mercan, the daughter of his enemy. Mercan, already promised to Nezir, is thrust into a battle she never chose, while Ateş binds her to him believing she is the key to his revenge. Yet the woman he intends to destroy becomes the one who threatens to undo him. And in the shadows, Aslı – driven by jealousy, seizes every chance to tear them apart.
cast: İlsu Demirci, Emin Günenç, Ezgi Dalgiç, Erol Yavan, Pervin Mert, Firat Kaya, Işıl Mete, Kaan Arkat
tagline: Bound By Fate

title: As If
overview: Yılmaz and İlkkan are two friends who are constantly fighting each other. Their biggest feature is always being able to do things that will turn their ordinary lives upside down. They magnify small events incredibly and give you a nervous breakdown. They are the embodiment of the misfortunes, strange events and despair we experience every day.
cast: Feyyaz Yiğit, Kıvanç Kılınç, Ahmet Kürşat Öçalan

title: Kelime Oyunu
overview: Kelime Oyunu (Word Game) is a Turkish game show that was awarded by the Turkish Language Association for "the correct use of Turkish and its contributions to Turkish" and broadcasted every weekday on Kanal 1, Bloomberg HT, Show TV, FOX and continues to be broadcast on teve2.
cast: Ali İhsan Varol

title: Valley of the Wolves: Ambush
overview: A continuation series of Osman Sinav's The Wolves Of The Valley. This produce focuses on politics, National Security and Regional Middle East problems. The Wolves of The Valley: Ambush serie also attracts attention by two film; The Wolves of The Valley: Iraq and The Wolves of The Valley: Palestine.
genres: Drama, War & Politics, Action & Adventure, Crime
cast: Necati Şaşmaz, Erhan Ufak, Yılmaz Meydaneri, Nevin Efe, Ayla Algan

title: Söz
overview: Yavuz Karasu, a well-trained Turkish soldier, is in Istanbul with his fiance where a terrorist operation takes place. Dr. Bahar, who is not a surgeon, tries to save his fiance. After this incident, Yavuz goes back to Karabayir and Bahar follows him all the way to give him something that belongs to him. Another incident happens and he is chosen to be the commander of a team whose mission is to catch a terrorist and protect Karabiyir and their country Turkey. The whole team is solid, consistent, and willing to sacrifice their lives for the sake of their country.
cast: Tolga Sarıtaş, Nihat Altınkaya, Deniz Baysal, Eren Vurdem, Burak Sevinç, Aytaç Şaşmaz, Ilayda Ildir, Meriç Aral

title: Bez Bebek
cast: Evrim Akın, Oya Aydoğan, Mehmet Usta, Ege Tanman, Asena Keskinci, Sevinç Aktansel, Alpay Atalan, Fatoş Kabasakal

title: Bandits Cannot Rule The World
overview: The series tells the life of Hizir Çakırbeyli. A mob boss who entered the realm of the mafia at a young age.
cast: Oktay Kaynarca, Yunus Emre Yıldırımer, Ozan Akbaba, Sabina Toziya, Hakan Karsak, Yiğit Uçan, Öner Ateş, Sevinç Gürşen Kıranlı

title: Mucize Doktor
overview: Mucize Doktor / A Miracle (2019) is a Turkish FOX medical drama (64 episodes) starring Taner Ölmez, adapted from the Korean series Good Doctor. Ali Vefa, a young surgical resident with autism savant syndrome, joins a prestigious Istanbul hospital — facing resistance from colleagues who doubt his abilities. As he proves his genius case by case, he navigates friendships, rivalry and first love while learning what it means to belong.
cast: Taner Ölmez, Onur Tuna, Sinem Ünsal, Hazal Türesan, Murat Aygen, Fırat Altunmeşe, Hakan Kurtaş, Hayal Köseoğlu

title: Mrs. Fazilet and Her Daughters
overview: The story of Fazilet, mother of Hazan and Ece, eager to make money using the modeling skills of her younger daughter, Ece. While Hazan pursues a career as a coach in sports, far from her mother's wishes and falls in love with a rich spoiled man.
cast: Nazan Kesal, Deniz Baysal, Çağlar Ertuğrul, Mahir Günşiray, Tolga Güleç, Hazal Türesan, Afra Saraçoğlu, İdris Nebi Taşkan
first_review: Great acting, non-forgettable love story, a lots of surprises!

title: Redemption
overview: Orhun is a successful businessman who was raised in a wealthy but loveless family along with his twin sister Nihan, younger sister Nurşah and their cold, distant mother Afife. Nihan left to do volunteer work as a doctor in Eritrea, but recently she has been impossible to contact, and her family is worried about her. While looking for his sister, he comes across Hira, a poor girl living the life of a prisoner at the hands of traffickers. When they find out that Nihan has been poisoned and killed by a local gang, Orhun breaks down. When it emerges that it was actually Hira who poisoned his sister, Orhun hatches an evil revenge plan. Meanwhile, Afife, their mother, has kept Orhun's father away since his childhood secretly. Orhun decides to marry Hira as revenge. Afife hates people of lower status and she’s obsessed with “pure blood”. Will this marriage imprison Afife , or will the couple find redemption after paying all their dues?
cast: Mahassine Merabet, Cenk Torun, Melahat Abbasova, Fulya Özcan, Pervin Mert, Ali Yağcı, Mustafa Şimşek

title: Beni Affet
cast: Nusret Şenay, Ceren Yalazoğlu Karakoç, Zeynep Aytek, Cansin Yilmaz, Damla Tombak, Özge Mirzalı, Şeyma Korkmaz, İlkay Kayku

title: A Woman Scorned
overview: Asya is a successful doctor and she has a perfect life with her husband and her son. But after finding a blonde hair on her husband's scarf, everything changes and she discovers her husband's extramarital affair.
cast: Cansu Dere, Caner Cindoruk, Melis Sezen, Eren Vurdem, Alp Akar, Taro Emir Tekin, Nazlı Bulum, Yeliz Kuvancı

title: The Family
overview: Aile is a story of Aslan who is the son of a large family, runs a nightclub. An extremely shrewd man. He is a dark and mysterious character. Psychologist Devin will enter the life of this difficult man and it becomes intense.
cast: Kıvanç Tatlıtuğ, Serenay Sarıkaya, Nur Sürer, Nejat İşler, Canan Ergüder, Yüsra Geyik, Umutcan Ütebay, Emel Göksu

title: Medcezir
overview: Yaman, who lives in the suburb of Tozludere (Istanbul), is arrested and put in jail with his brother because his brother decided to steal a car at a petrol station. Until that day he tried to change his destiny by working hard to earn a good life. That evening he was unwillingly introduced to crime and his clean dossier was tainted. He meets a rich lawyer, Selim Serez.
cast: Çağatay Ulusoy, Serenay Sarıkaya, Metin Akdülger, Taner Ölmez, Hazar Ergüçlü, Barış Falay, Şebnem Dönmez, Murat Aygen

title: Payitaht: Abdulhamid
overview: The fight of Abdülhamid II to keep the Ottoman Empire and Caliphate alive.
cast: Bülent İnal, Duygu Gürcan, Hakan Boyav, Bahadır Yenişehirlioğlu, Taner Ertürkler, Halil Kumova, Ercan Demirel, Yusuf Aytekin

title: Mother
overview: Anne (Mother), a 2016–2017 Turkish drama, follows Zeynep, a substitute teacher who discovers her 7-year-old student, Melek, is being severely abused and neglected by her mother, Sule. To save her, Zeynep kidnaps Melek, fakes her death, and attempts to become her adoptive mother, leading to an emotional, high-stakes battle for custody and safety.
cast: Cansu Dere, Vahide Perçin, Beren Gökyıldız, Gonca Vuslateri, Berkay Ateş, Gülenay Kalkan, Can Nergis, Alize Gördüm

title: Prophet Joseph
overview: This story basically follows the most important happenings in Prophet Josephs life, from the view of muslims. The most important happenings are: 1. his travel to egypt 2. his rise and growing up in Egypt 3. his life problems when his "owner" Zuleikha falls in love with him and wants him to return the love to him. 4. his stay in prison, and how he reforms the prisoners. 5. his return by the side of the Pharao. 6. his uprising to the side of the pharao as his advisor. 7. his brothers coming to egypt, and how he decides to make them regret their mistakes so they can receive forgivness, and peace. 8. his reunion with his family after more than 20 years.
cast: Mostafa Zamani, Katayoun Riahi, Mahmoud Pakniat, Amir Hossein Modarres, Rahim Norouzi, Jahanbakhsh Soltani, Parvaneh Massoumi, Jafar Dehghan
first_review: In fact this series is amazing in one word. Whenever I watch this series I am transported to another world. I forget that this is a documentary film. I feel like I have left the era of Yusuf (as). I don't know that era. How was it? And everyone's character has been developed in such a way that the whole thing seems to be real in this series.But in one word I would say this is the best documentary

title: Children of Paradise
overview: Iskender was a feared gangster in the dark alleys of Istanbul, known for his own rough code of honor—until betrayal left him bleeding and stripped of everything he had built. When he opens his eyes in a quiet Aegean town, he finds himself surrounded by warmth he has never known: a caring mother, a blind little sister, a home that feels like it was always his. The townspeople believe he is Kamil, a long-lost son returned; only he knows the truth. But the past never lets go—old enemies, blood feuds, and the ghosts of his choices haunt him still. As he uncovers a shocking secret—that this family is bound to him by blood—Iskender stands at a crossroads. Revenge or redemption? Will he remain the ruthless survivor the streets created, or dare to become the man he was meant to be? This is a story of love, betrayal, and the price of becoming truly human.
cast: İsmail Hacıoğlu, Özgü Kaya, Yurdaer Okur, Melisa Şenolsun, Şebnem Doğruer, Evliya Aykan, Eda Akalın, Ali Gözüşirin

title: Resurrection: Ertugrul
overview: Ertuğrul Bey and the Knights Templar in the 13th century Alba and step and step with the struggle against brutal Mongols depicts the process of establishing the Ottoman principality.
cast: Engin Altan Düzyatan, Hülya Darcan, Didem Balçın, Cengiz Coşkun, Nurettin Sönmez, Celal Al, Edip Zeydan, Ayberk Pekcan

title: Envy
overview: Deprived of her mother's attention and love throughout her life, Seniha grows up with a deep sense of jealousy towards her older brother, Halit. For her beauty-obsessed mother, Seniha is the ugly duckling, completely unfit for her noble and wealthy family. Her older brother, Halit, is the opposite. He is her mother's pride in every sense. Halit attends the best schools and takes advantage of the family's resources. He becomes both the country's most successful lawyer and its most promiscuous bachelor. Seniha, however, sinks deeper and deeper into the depths of her sense of worthlessness. She never finished school, never married, and has practically become a servant in her own home. She has become, as her older brother calls her, "Miss Nobody.
cast: Özgü Namal, Selahattin Paşalı, Mehmet Günsür, Ayda Aksel, Beril Pozam, Hafsanur Sancaktutan, Hande Doğandemir, Dilara Aksüyek

title: Güldür Güldür Show
overview: Güldür Güldür Show, which has won the admiration of everyone with its legendary cast, continues to bring a new approach to many topics from family to love, fame to technology, football to friendship with its unique sense of humour.
cast: Ali Sunal, Doğa Rutkay, Onur Buldu, Onur Atilla, Uğur Bilgin, Meltem Yılmazkaya, Aylin Kontente, Erdem Yener

title: Hercai
overview: Miran is seeking revenge for his parents death so he plans to marry daughter Reyyan from the family who were responsible for that. Unexpectedly he falls in love with Reyyan. Will this change the plan?
cast: Ebru Şahin, Akin Akinözü, Ayda Aksel, Macit Sonkan, Gülçin Santırcıoğlu, Serhat Tutumluer, Oya Unustası, Ahmet Tansu Taşanlar

title: Magnificent Century: Kösem
overview: Tells the legendary story between the Sultan of Kosem and Ahmed I.
cast: Nurgül Yeşilçay, İsmail Demirci, Ece Çeşmioğlu, Burcu Gül Kazbek, İdil Yener, Sibel Taşçıoğlu, Engin Benli, Leyla Feray

title: My Name Is Farah
overview: A murder unintentionally witnessed by Farah, who had to flee her country and illegally struggle to survive with her son, and Tahir Lekesiz, whom she met on the same night, will change their lives forever.
cast: Demet Özdemir, Engin Akyürek, Fırat Tanış, Feyyaz Duman, Burcu Türünz, Hatice Aslan, Sera Kutlubey, Burak Tamdoğan
first_review: Adim Farah is one of, if not best new Turkish series. Engin Akyurek and Demet Ozdemir are excellent! Based on “The Cleaner” but has its own storyline. Well-written. All actors in series are wonderful including Rastin Pahadar who plays Kerim. Watch it!

title: Love for Rent
overview: When Defne's brother gets into debt and is held captive, Defne is forced to accept an offer in exchange for the money. She has to make Ömer, a wealthy shoe designer who owns a company, fall in love with her and marry her.
cast: Elçin Sangu, Barış Arduç, Salih Bademci, Nergis Kumbasar, Levent Ülgen, Kerem Fırtına, Sanem Yeles, Onur Büyüktopçu

title: Three Sisters
overview: Once upon a time in a beautiful yet mysterious country, there lived three sisters named Türkan, Dönüş, and Derya. As a close-knit family, they would dream of a promising future every night. Perhaps such an upbringing, that was similar to a fairy tale on the pine-scented streets of Ayvalık, had not prepared them for the grim reality of adulthood. Over time, the sisters discover that life and the choices that are accompanied by it are not as easy as they seem. Inconceivable secrets can be hidden even by those who are trusted the most and horrific illnesses can lead to the resurrection of the past. This leads them to the question: Is there anything in the world that time cannot heal?
cast: İclal Aydın, Reha Özcan, Almila Ada, Berker Güven, Benian Dönmez, Nazlı Senem Ünal, Hakan Atalay, Vural Şahanoğlu

title: Çocuklar Duymasın
cast: Pınar Altuğ, Tamer Karadağlı, Zeyno Günenç, Meriç Acemi, Ferdi Akarnur, Bengisu Özenel, Melek Şahin, Yakup Uysal

title: Konuşanlar
overview: This chat show is very different from what you have seen in the past. Every person has a different story, every story has a different side. Hasan Can Kaya first chooses the speakers and then the fun increases with interesting questions.
cast: Hasan Can Kaya

title: Deniz Inside My Heart
overview: Deniz has everything a woman can ask for. But when her husband disappeared no trace, this happy life changes. Deniz, who is in a desperate state with her two children and her father, meets Mirat who will be a lover and be a life-saver.
cast: Özge Özberk, Kutsi, Yeşim Ceren Bozoğlu, Hakan Eratik, Devin Özgür Çınar, Hakan Vanlı, Hazal Adıyaman, Temmuz Uğur Yıldız

title: Eshq Mamnou'
overview: Having lost his wife eleven years ago, Adnan devoted all his attention to his daughter Nihal and his son Bülent. Adnan, who lives in one of the most prominent mansions along the Bosporus in Istanbul with a relative's son Behlül, and his children's nanny, meets Bihter who is also a socialite couple's daughter she is very elegant and beautiful, everyone who meets her get stunned and jealous of her, and he falls in love with her. While searching for peace, safety and happiness in Adnan's mansion, Bihter meets passion. Behlül and Bihter fall completely into each other and become drowned by their infatuation with each other. Their secret love affair will soon affect every member of the family.
cast: Beren Saat, Selçuk Yöntem, Kıvanç Tatlıtuğ, Nebahat Çehre, Zerrin Tekindor, Hazal Kaya, Batuhan Karacakaya, Rana Cabbar

title: Wounded Love
overview: Azize finds herself with her 3 children and mother-in-law in a difficult fight. She raises her children while fighting the difficulties of the war period and her husband's absence whom she loves more than anything. It's about war, love, greed, treason and sacrifice.
cast: Halit Ergenç, Bergüzar Korel, Onur Saylak, Okan Yalabık, Devrim Özkan, Celile Toyon, Fatih Artman, Boran Kuzum
first_review: Beautiful, moving story. Great acting, beautiful love story.

title: Bitter Lands
overview: The love story of Zuleyha, ​​who grew up without a father and works with Yilmaz as an auxiliary weaver in order to earn money for her brother, a gambler. The lives of these two young people blinded by the love of one ordinary night will turn into a dark nightmare. This nightmare will take them first from Istanbul and far away, and later to the estate of the Yaman family. In the first episode of the series, Yilmaz and Zuleyha set out to work on the Yaman estate. However, because of one of Zuleyha's lies - the lives of all of them will change radically.
cast: Hilal Altınbilek, Kerem Alışık, Bülent Polat, Selin Yeninci, Sibel Taşçıoğlu, Selin Genç, Polen Emre, Serpil Tamur

title: Tatlı Kaçıklar
cast: Mehmet Ali Erbil, Yalçın Menteş, Buket Dereoğlu, Kayhan Yıldızoğlu, Erol Günaydın, İsmet Ay, Dursun Ali Sarıoğlu, Ufuk Yetik

title: Behzat Ç.: An Ankara Policeman
overview: Centered around a personally troubled, officially discredited police chief and his inharmonious police team in Ankara Police Force. Even though the show usually focuses on murders and other crimes that happens on a weekly basis, the undertone of the story leads to an eventual duel between Behzat Ç. and the rotten department officials and bureaucrats.
genres: Drama, War & Politics, Action & Adventure, Crime
cast: Erdal Beşikçioğlu, Canan Ergüder, Ege Aydan, İnanç Konukçu, Berkan Şal, Serdar Orçin, Gökhan Yıkılkan, Eray Eserol

title: Time Goes By
overview: Set in the 1960s, the story of the Akarsu family includes Ali, Cemile and their four children: Berrin, a law student, Aylin and Mete, high school students, and 5 year old Osman. Ali begins an affair with Caroline, a Dutch woman, which leads to the disintegration of the family.
cast: Ayça Bingöl, Wilma Elles, Yıldız Çağrı Atiksoy, Aras Bulut Iynemli, Gün Koper, Farah Zeynep Abdullah, Meral Çetinkaya, Mete Horozoğlu

title: Black Rose
overview: A married women has to fight for the things she lost because of the revealed secrets of her husband.
cast: Ece Uslu, Yavuz Bingöl, Özlem Conker, Mesut Akusta, Sebahat Kumaş, Ogün Kaptanoğlu, Hülya Duyar, Hilal Altınbilek

title: What is Fatmagul's Fault?
overview: Fatmagul, a simple happy village girl, is violated by 4 drunk rich young men, the extreme hardship she faces forced to marry a rapist and the great remorse felt by others for their inaction when innocence was violated forms the story.
cast: Beren Saat, Engin Akyürek, Buğra Gülsoy, Kaan Taşaner, Engin Öztürk, Fırat Çelik, Sumru Yavrucuk, Esra Dermancıoğlu
first_review: It's amazing series also Engin Akyürek is amazing and handsome actor

title: Back Streets
overview: The series tells the story of the family lives of the police officers working in a special team of the Istanbul Police Department's Public Security Department and their adventures on the streets of Istanbul. The team encounters different and diverse human stories during their missions. The Istanbul Police Chief Rıza Soylu (Zafer Ergin), with his years of experience and fatherhood, approaches these stories, which at times make them smile and at other times are heartbreaking, and guides the other young members of the team.
genres: Action & Adventure, Crime, Drama, Family, Mystery, Comedy
cast: Zafer Ergin, Şevket Çoruh, Özgür Ozan, Oya Okar, Ozan Çobanoğlu, Alp Korkmaz, Ebru Cündübeyoğlu, Sarp Levendoğlu
first_review: The Best Of All time

title: My Home My Destiny
overview: Doğduğun Ev Kaderindir (My Home My Destiny / منزلي) is a 2019 Turkish romantic drama based on a true story. Born into poverty and given up for adoption at age seven, Zeynep grows into an educated, beautiful young woman living a fairytale life — until her biological mother reappears, threatening to drag her back to the world she left behind. Two wounded souls, Zeynep and Mehdi, find themselves entangled in a love story shaped entirely by the homes that made them. Starring Demet Özdemir and İbrahim Çelikkol.
cast: Demet Özdemir, İbrahim Çelikkol, Zuhal Gencer, Senan Kara, Hülya Duyar, Elif Sönmez, Fatih Koyunoğlu, Engin Öztürk

title: Be My Sunshine
overview: The paths of city businesswoman Haziran and Poyraz, a handsome and helpful young man, intersect on Swallow Island, and love arises from the disagreements of these two opposite characters.
cast: Ayça Ayşin Turan, Alp Navruz, Nihan Büyükağaç, İpek Tenolcay, Bülent Çolak, Rami Narin, Merve Nur Bengi, Fatih Yücebağ

title: My Name is Melek
overview: Melek will rebuild her life with her family and children. Melek is struggling to survive with her three children.
cast: Nehir Erdoğan, Kaan Çakır, Mehmet Çevik, Şerif Sezer, Rabia Soytürk, Ulvi Kahyaoğlu, Poyraz Ar, Kutsi
first_review: This is a very well written and well paced show. Right from the first few episodes you can tell that the writers and director have crafted a strong vision for the show and the actors have become their characters. By the end of the first few episodes you are hooked and this becomes the perfect gateway into other Turkish series.

title: Valley of Hearts
overview: Sumru left the children she gave birth to at a young age and built a new life and family for herself. Melek and Nuh learn their mother's identity from their grandmother's final words and set out to find her. Sumru, who is now married to one of Cappadocia's wealthiest businessmen, Samet, continues her lavish life with the children she had with him. However, she has different plans regarding Samet's son from his first marriage, Cihan. The paths of Melek and Nuh, who are seeking to confront their mother, will unexpectedly cross with Cihan and his reluctant fiancée, Sevilay.
cast: Ece Uslu, Burak Sergen, Burak Tozkoparan, Leyla Tanlar, Hafsanur Sancaktutan, Aras Aydın, Genco Özak, Esra Dermancıoğlu
tagline: Fire and gunpowder do not stay in the same place. If they do, we will be reduced to ashes!

title: Old Money
overview: Nihal's affluent life comes under threat when a self-made tycoon with an eye for money — and an even sharper one for love — shakes up Istanbul's elite.
cast: Engin Akyürek, Aslı Enver, Dolunay Soysert, İsmail Demirci, Serkan Altunorak, Taro Emir Tekin, Zeynep Oymak, Ahmet Utlu

title: El-Turk
overview: Balaban, a soldier in the Janissary army, settles in Moena in northern Italy after the Battle of Vienna in 1683 and fights for the rights of the local people.
cast: Can Yaman, Greta Ferro, Will Kemp, Magnus Samuelsson, Slavko Sobin, Haluk Piyes, Madalena Aragão, David Streames
tagline: He has to fight for the right to love.

title: I Am Mother
overview: It follows a woman in an abusive marriage who sends her son to Istanbul for care. The son goes missing, and after three years, she meets him again, but he now goes by a different name.
cast: Özge Özpirinçci, Demet Akbağ, Mina Akdin, Meriç Aral, Uğur Yücel, Nalan Okçuoğlu, Sarp Kaan Altınçapa, Miray Yeral

title: Ezel
overview: Ömer, a young man returning from military service, is set up by his friends and fiancé and ends up in prison for ten years. He fakes his own death and manages to escape. He re-creates himself as ‘Ezel’, a high-end gambler who is outwardly a successful man, but inwardly driven by one thing - his determination to understand why the people he loved betrayed him, and take his revenge.
cast: Kenan İmirzalıoğlu, Cansu Dere, Yiğit Özşener, Barış Falay, Sarp Akkaya, Tuncel Kurtiz, Güray Kip, Bade Işcil

title: Mevlana Rumi
overview: In 13th-century Anatolia, as the Mongol threat looms and internal turmoil rages, Rumi, a wise spiritual figure, emerges to assuage people's fears. His timeless words unite reason and compassion, inspiring change.

title: The Ambassador's Daughter
overview: Sancar has always & Nare has always yearned for love. When they meet and fall in love as children, they know they are meant to be; they just have to wait to be old enough to marry. On the night of their wedding, a startling revelation by Nare forever changes their lives and puts their love on hold until the night of another wedding many years later.
cast: Engin Akyürek, Tuba Büyüküstün, Erdal Küçükkömürcü, Gonca Cilasun, Doğukan Polat, Hivda Zizan Alp, Edip Tepeli, Beren Gençalp

title: The Prince
overview: Following the death of the king, the youngest and least loved royal son -- known simply as “Prince” -- assumes the throne in the Kingdom of Bongomia.
cast: Giray Altınok, Ceyda Düvenci, Serdar Orçin, Çağdaş Onur Öztürk, Onur Özaydın, Bahadır Vatanoğlu, Derya Pınar Ak, Canberk Gültekin

title: Love Makes Us Cry
overview: Young people who migrated from the provinces to Istanbul and tried to survive alone; it is the story of the conflict between their conscience and their love.
cast: Deniz Can Aktaş, Hafsanur Sancaktutan, Yağız Can Konyalı, Pelin Öztekin, Eda Şölenci, Oktay Çubuk, Sezin Bozacı, Durul Bazan

title: Sıfır Bir
overview: The story of a group of young people who live in Adana – Hürriyet District got involved in illegal businesses and became Adana's strongest gang over time. The series, which started as an independent production in 2016 and broadcast on Youtube, has been transferred to Blutv with all its episodes as of the 3rd season.
cast: Savaş Satış, Onur Akbay, Burak Şahin, Burak Şafak, Cengizhan Yurdutek, Burak Akyüz, İrfan Ballı, Emrullah Sürmeli

title: Our Story
overview: Our Story / Bizim Hikaye (2017) is a Turkish FOX drama (70 episodes, 2 seasons) starring Hazal Kaya and Burak Deniz, adapted from Shameless. Twenty-year-old Filiz has sacrificed everything — school, dreams, youth — to raise her five younger siblings after their mother abandoned them and their alcoholic father Fikri proves useless. When mysterious Barış enters her life and falls hard for her, Filiz must decide if love has any place in her impossible world.
cast: Hazal Kaya, Burak Deniz, Reha Özcan, Yağız Can Konyalı, Zeynep Selimoğlu, Alp Akar, Ömer Sevgi, Mehmetcan Mincinözlü

title: Deeply
overview: The stories of love – and heartbreak – of various couples, from their memorable beginnings to their inevitable ends.
cast: Timuçin Esen, Bergüzar Korel, Cemal Toktas, Gizem Erdem, Durukan Çelikkaya, Mustafa Enis Bilir, Aydan Taş

title: The Girl Named Feriha
overview: Adını Feriha Koydum is a Turkish television drama series produced by Med Yapım. The series broadcast on Show TV and it is written by duo Melis Civelek and Sırma Yanık.
cast: Çağatay Ulusoy, Metin Çekmez, Deniz Uğur, Yusuf Akgün, Ceyda Ateş, Sedef Şahin, Feyza Civelek, Zeynep Çamcı

title: Sila
overview: A young woman's forced marriage to the agha of a tribe in Southeastern Turkey leads to tragedy and social change.
cast: Cansu Dere, Mehmet Akif Alakurt, Namık Kemal Yiğittürk, Fatoş Tez, Menderes Samancılar, Zeynep Eronat, Devrim Saltoğlu, Kartal Balaban

title: Chrysalis
overview: Nalan, a kind and brilliant architect working for Turkey's largest hotel chain, carries a secret no one knows. Sedat, the company's wealthy heir, has spent years in a forbidden affair with an older socialite — until his powerful father forces him into marriage to protect the family empire. Two strangers bound by obligation, each hiding wounds from a painful past. Will their unlikely union blossom into something extraordinary, or will the darkness they've buried consume them both?
cast: Burcu Biricik, Feyyaz Şerifoğlu, Hande Ataizi, Selma Ergeç, Nur Sürer, Tamer Levent, Enis Arıkan, Hamza Yazıcı

title: Saladın: The Conqueror of Jerusalem
overview: The television series centers on the life of Salahudddin, a 12th-century Muslim ruler, and his conquest of Jerusalem. It also delves into his challenges and conflicts against the Crusaders, as well as his ambition to unify the Muslim territories of Syria, northern Mesopotamia, Palestine, and Egypt under his leadership.
cast: Uğur Güneş, Mehmet Ali Nuroğlu, Sezgin Erdemir, Erdinç Gülener, Seda Yıldız, Ali Ersan Duru, Zeynep Kumral, Egemen Yavuz
tagline: Conqueror of Jerusalem

title: Survivor Türkiye
overview: Survivor Türkiye is the Turkish version of the popular reality Survivor. This version of the show has aired on both Kanal D in 2005, and on Show TV in 2006, 2007, and 2010. From the beginning Survivor was a success in Turkey, however the cost of producing the show proved to be too much for Kanal D and even Show TV couldn't afford to produce the show on a yearly basis. Because of production costs the show was put on hiatus in 2007 and was brought back three years later. The prize for the first seasons of the show was 150,000 euros, for the second and third seasons 250,000 euros, and 500,000 dollars for the fourth season.
cast: Acun Ilıcalı, Murat Ceylan Wish, Büşra Yalçın, Tuğçe Melis Demir, Barış Murat Yağcı, Bayhan, Deniz Çatalbaş, Mert Nobre

title: Prisoner of Love
overview: After his mother left his father for another man, Ömer’s perception of women was forever damaged, and he considers them unreliable, untrustworthy and even dangerous. The only woman he has time for is his beloved sister Ayse, for whom he would do anything. Ayse herself is a great romantic and wants nothing more than for her brother to be happy and find love for himself. When she falls ill, and with only months to live, Ömer understands that the only way for his sister to die happy is for him to be married
cast: Cenk Torun, Deniz Durmaz, Sabire Feyza Kaya, Çağan Alp Önder, Ali Çoban, Dünya Gündoğdu, Gökberk Demirci, Tuğçe Ersoy

title: Twist of Fate
overview: Ada is a young girl who believes that if she is abandoned by her first love, she will be unhappy forever like the women of her maternal side. While trying to escape the fate of her family, Ada falls in love with Rüzgar, a student from Albania at the same university who is threatened with deportation. Believing that Rüzgar is her first love, Ada proposes him and they have a fake marriage to prevent his deportation. She believes that Rüzgar will literally propose to her on the 3rd anniversary of their fake marriage and then their real marriage adventure will finally begin. But unfortunately, all the dreams and hopes of Ada will be ruined when she is abandoned by Rüzgar on their 3rd wedding anniversary. Ada immediately takes action to get her first love back. But fate has its own way and will put Bora in Ada's way at an unexpected moment... A workaholic, callous, self-righteous alpha male with tightly closed doors for love...
cast: Aytaç Şaşmaz, Cemre Baysel, İdris Nebi Taşkan, Aslı Sümen, Hande Subaşı, Funda İlhan, Tuğba Çom, Anıl Çelik

title: Power of Love
overview: A group of competitive women and men come together in The Power of Love to find their dream partner. Every week, some contestants win the weekly prize, while others say goodbye to their dreams of love and the house. Emotional moments, surprises, and exciting challenges await in this thrilling season!
cast: Öykü Serter, Ayla Nur Tekcanlı, Belis Kılıçarslan, Buket Şenal, Büşra Nur Bulut, Feyza Nur Ceylan, Melek Cambaz, Perinaz Şiri

title: Insider
overview: Even though two brothers are completely separated from each other after the imprisoning of their father and the kidnapping of their twin brother, they meet by coincidence years after when both choose to enter the police academy.
cast: Çağatay Ulusoy, Aras Bulut Iynemli, Çetin Tekindor, Nihal Koldaş, Damla Colbay, Bensu Soral, Yıldıray Şahinler, Mustafa Uğurlu

title: A.B.I.: A Broken Inheritance
overview: Dogan is a successful surgeon in Izmir. When he was young, a shocking incident with his father, Tahir Hancıoglu, caused him to break away from his family. Now devoted to saving lives, Dogan has almost no connection with his family except his younger sister, Melek. At Melek’s insistence, Dogan returns to Istanbul for his sister Gulusan’s wedding. His return brings back long-buried traumas and hidden crimes. Dogan meets Cagla, a strong, determined lawyer. Their painful pasts create a deep connection. The family’s enemy, Behram, watches from the shadows, waiting for the right moment to confront the past. An attempted murder on the wedding night changes everything. Old secrets surface and members of his family appear among the suspects. Dogan must make a choice: leave and return to his old life, or stay and face the truth by uncovering the secrets at the heart of his family.
cast: Kenan İmirzalıoğlu, Afra Saraçoğlu, Asude Kalebek, Sinan Tuzcu, Diren Polatoğulları, Tarık Papuççuoğlu, Tülay Bursa, Nihal Koldaş

title: Bir Küçük Gün Işığı
overview: Elif is very much in love with Hakan. The only thing missing from their marriage is a child. While Elif is thinking about starting the adoption procedure, she learns that Hakan had an accident. And his life begins to change irreversibly. Fırat, on the other hand, finds the trace of his brother Dila, who has been missing for years, and brings him back home. Fırat is left between her mother Ümran, who is prescriptive and oppressive, and her rebellious and Decadent brother Dila. While Elif’s life is turned upside down, Euphrates is unaware of the web that fate has woven for her. The path of Elif and Euphrates intersects in a way that they will never be separated again.
cast: Seray Kaya, Berk Oktay, Esra Dermancıoğlu, Tuğçe Açıkgöz, Şeyma Korkmaz, Yilmaz Bayraktar, Güneş Hayat, Gizem Ünsal

title: Ramo
overview: The series will focus on the conflict between the two families and a great love that will flourish between this struggle.
cast: Murat Yildirim, Esra Bilgiç, Görkem Sevindik, İlker Aksum, Yiğit Özşener, İdil Fırat, Rüzgar Aksoy, İlkay Kayku

title: Woman
overview: The story of mother Bahar, who carries the weight and love of two children in their hearts with enthusiasm in their hearts. Bahar, abandoned by her mother when she was eight years old, later lost her grandma and her father. When she thinks she is all alone, she meets Sarp, whom she delicately loves.
cast: Özge Özpirinçci, Caner Cindoruk, Bennu Yıldırımlar, Seray Kaya, Ece Özdikici, Feyyaz Duman, Devrim Özder Akın, Gökçe Eyüboğlu

title: Last Summer
overview: Idealist Public Prosecutor Selim Kara receives an offer he cannot refuse from the organized crime leader Selçuk Taşkın, whom he sent to prison 8 years ago. Selçuk Taşkın wants to testify in one of Prosecutor Selim's cases and his testimony will destroy the whole criminal organization. However, there is only one condition for this; the personal protection of his son, Akgün Gökalp Taşkın.
cast: Ali Atay, Alperen Duymaz, Yaprak Medine, Hafsanur Sancaktutan, Birce Akalay, Arif Pişkin, Zuhal Gencer, Halil Babür

title: Love, Reason, Get Even
overview: Esra divorced Ozan when his startup dreams left them broke — eight years later she discovers he's now a wildly successful tech billionaire and schemes to get close to him by becoming an intern at his company.
cast: Burcu Özberk, İlhan Şen, Mehmet Korhan Fırat, Günay Karacaoğlu, Zeynep Kankonde, Ceren Koç, Mehmet Yilmaz, Suleyman Atanisev

title: Price Of Passion
overview: Ferhat is a hitman working for his criminal uncle. Aslı is a young and idealistic doctor. One day, their roads crossed in the most unexpected way: Aslı is forced to operate on a man that Ferhat had shot.
cast: İbrahim Çelikkol, Birce Akalay, Muhammet Uzuner, Arzu Gamze Kılınç, Ece Dizdar, Deniz Celiloğlu, Cahit Gök, Uğur Aslan

title: The European Side
overview: The life and times of rather traditional Sutcuoglu family and their comedic struggles to adapt the high-profile contemporary life of Nisantasi.
cast: Gazanfer Özcan, Gülse Birsel, Engin Günaydın, Ata Demirer, Levent Üzümcü, Rutkay Aziz, Suna Keskin, Şenay Gürler

title: Seksenler
overview: Turkey's series about the events experienced in the 1980s
cast: Özlem Türkad, Şoray Uzun, İlker Ayrık, Ayşe Tolga, Berat Yenilmez, Necmi Yapıcı, Tahsin Taşkın, Ceyhun Fersoy

title: Aşk ve Gurur
overview: A wealthy family faces bankruptcy and must marry off one of their three daughters to Tolga, a seemingly rich heir. The eldest daughter resists despite mutual attraction, while her sister pursues him. Unknown to all, Tolga is also broke.
cast: Deniz Çatalbaş, Batuhan Demir, Ceylan Batı, Ali Pınar, Lara Aksüt

title: Tozkoparan İskender: Kaos
cast: Çağan Efe Ak, Berat Efe Parlar, Yusuf Gökhan Atalay, Can Sipahi, Yagiz Kilinc, Durul Bazan, Tuana Naz Tiryaki, Bensu Uğur

title: Thank You, Next
overview: After a painful breakup, a young lawyer dives head first into the confusing world of modern dating, with the unwavering support of her best friends.

title: Leyla and Mecnun
overview: Leyla ile Mecnun is a Turkish television comedy series. The show is set in Istanbul, Turkey and premiered in 2011 on TRT. The series is a surreal and absurd comedy that revolves around the fictional love story between Leyla and Mecnun.
cast: Ali Atay, Serkan Keskin, Ahmet Mümtaz Taylan, Cengiz Bozkurt, Osman Sonant, Köksal Engür, Nalan Kuruçim, İştar Gökseven

title: Kanıt
overview: Each episode, police inspectors Orhan and Selim try and solve puzzling cases with the help and guidance of forensics. Professor Sevil Atasoy, a forensic scientist, also joins us sometimes to tell relevant anecdotes.
cast: Sevil Atasoy, Engin Benli, Deniz Celiloğlu, Ece Güzel, Kubilay Güleçoğlu

title: Shahrzad
overview: A romantic historical drama set around and after the 1953 Iranian coup d'état.
cast: Taraneh Alidoosti, Shahab Hosseini, Mostafa Zamani, Parinaz Izadyar, Mehdi Soltani, Parviz Fallahipour, Houman Barghnavard, Gelare Abbasi
tagline: In the tempest of circumstance love is the first victim.

title: RU
overview: Ryan and Ozir: twenty years separate them, but an unconventional love brings them together in the small town of Aegean, where their hearts defy the boundaries of age and society and cling to their hope for happiness.
cast: Meryem Uzerli, Burak Berkay Akgül, Hira Koyuncuoğlu, Gökay Türkmen, Zafer Algöz, Emre Karayel, Kartal Can Ermiş, Beril Kayar

title: Red Roses
overview: “Kızıl Goncalar” tells the poignant story of Meryem, married off at 14 in an Islamic sect, and now battling bravely to prevent her daughter Zeynep from suffering the same fate as the bride of the sheikh’s grandson. Guided by her sister-in-law, a former sect member, they realize they must flee to save Zeynep from the sect’s powerful clutches. Fearfully hiding her plans from her cruel husband, Meryem must first earn money as a carer in the home of secular psychiatrist Levent, who agrees to help her. Despite their clashing religious and progressive beliefs, Meryem and Levent are drawn together emotionally, but the turmoil in their lives deepens as the identity of his daughter is revealed.
cast: Özgü Namal, Özcan Deniz, Erkan Avcı, Mert Turak, Mert Yazıcıoğlu, Selen Öztürk, Şerif Erol, Duygu Sarışın

title: Love 101
overview: While trying to make their teacher fall for a basketball coach, four misfits and a model student find friendship, love and the courage to be themselves.
cast: Alina Boz, Mert Yazıcıoğlu, Kubilay Aka, Selahattin Paşalı, İpek Filiz Yazıcı, Pınar Deniz, Müfit Kayacan, Kaan Urgancıoğlu

title: We'll Be Fine
overview: Aktan and Lal, two polar opposites who meet at a party, find themselves caught up in a grand love story. Their fairytale romance spirals into chaos as they change everything in their lives to be together.
cast: Mert Ramazan Demir, Miray Daner, Olgu Baran Kubilay, İdil Sivritepe, Sercan Badur, Derin Beşikçioğlu, Ece Sükan, Yilmaz Bayraktar

title: Love Doesn't Understand Words
overview: Hayat is a country girl with strict parents. She is in a love hate relationship with her boss Murat. Hayat is full of secrets that can ruin her career and relationship.
cast: Hande Erçel, Burak Deniz, Özcan Tekdemir, Merve Çağıran, Oğuzhan Karbi, Demet Gül, Süleyman Felek, Betül Çobanoğlu

title: Black Money Love
overview: Ömer is a police officer. After the death of his fiancée, he suffers great pain. The body of Sibel, Ömer's fiancée, was found on the top of a cliff, in a car next to a fairly old, rich business man. After the shock of her sudden death and the accusations of his love cheating on him, Ömer realizes that there is more behind her suspicious murder
cast: Engin Akyürek, Tuba Büyüküstün, Erkan Can, Saygın Soysal, Burak Tamdoğan, Bestemsu Özdemir, Elif İnci, Ali Yörenç

title: Don't Let Go of My Hand
overview: The story revolves around a girl named Azra who is studying abroad in America to be a professional cook like her father. She met Cenk, the grandson of Ferida Celen, on the plane and took his bag instead of hers at the airport by mistake. Cenk who was expelled from his university in America and struggling to choose his own destiny will get to witness the huge turn in Azra's life when her father dies in a fire accident leaving her and his autistic son with his greedy wife.
cast: Alina Boz, Alp Navruz, Dolunay Soysert, Seray Gözler, Emre Bey, Ebru Aykaç, Burak Tamdoğan, Hazal Şenel

title: The Gift
overview: A painter in Istanbul embarks on a personal journey as she unearths universal secrets about an Anatolian archaeological site and its link to her past.
cast: Beren Saat, Mehmet Günsür, Melisa Şenolsun, Civan Canova, Tim Seyfi, Metin Akdülger, Başak Köklükaya, Senan Kara

title: Lost in Love
overview: Mete and Naz are the favorite children of rich and powerful families. Both of them are spoiled and used to getting everything they want in life. Their story, which started with the promise of their families in their childhood, flows towards marriage today with their inability to break away from each other. But when Naz's maid İncila, who has been with him since childhood, suddenly appears in front of Mete, Mete questions all the decisions he has made in life. Will this encounter between İncila and Mete change the direction of the story Naz wrote with all her stubbornness and help Mete find true love?
cast: Uraz Kaygılaroğlu, Cemre Baysel, Asude Kalebek, Şenay Gürler, Nilüfer Açıkalın, Açelya Devrim Yılhan, Edip Saner, Tamer Levent

title: Akasya Durağı
overview:
Akasya Durağı is a Turkish comedy television series on Kanal D, which initially broadcast in 2008

The Story:

Nuri is a very old driver and when he become retired he start his own taxi company with money that earned for all of his life. There are a few sympathetic driver and pure tea maker-office boy at the taxi company. And all of them life is very interesting and very funny. They have great time at the taxi company. Too many events do and find our drivers and their lives'. The story goes so... By the way, everyone says to Nuri 'Nuri Dad' because he is a very good man.
cast: Zeki Alasya, Cezmi Baskın, Levent Ülgen, Onur Şan, Nilgün Belgün, Aslı Altaylar, Timur Acar, Gülay Baltacı

title: The Genius
overview: Devran, a mathematical prodigy, has made a groundbreaking discovery in his doctoral thesis. He plans to go to U.S with his childhood love, Esme, after getting a university scholarship. However, the only person Devran wants to impress with this success is his father Iskender, who abandoned his family years ago. While protecting his family from Iskender, Devran struggles to stop his younger brother, Boran, from getting involved with their father, who already has plans for Boran. Devran’s plans have to change after Boran enters Iskender’s world.
cast: Aras Bulut Iynemli, Uğur Polat, Taner Ölmez, Onur Saylak, Melis Sezen, Ahsen Eroğlu, Seda Akman, Zuhal Gencer

title: Museum of Innocence
overview: In 1970s Istanbul, a man's forbidden love for a shop-girl evolves into a lifelong journey of obsession and longing.
cast: Selahattin Paşalı, Eylül Lize Kandemir, Oya Unustası, Tilbe Saran, Bülent Emin Yarar, Gülçin Kültür Şahin, Zeynep Dinsel, Tolga İskit

title: Golden Butterfly Awards
cast: Çağla Şıkel, Cem Davran

title: Al Maharib
overview: Savaşçı (المحارب / The Warrior) is a 2017 Turkish military action drama following Turkey's elite Maroon Berets special forces unit — known as the Sword Team. After their commanders are unjustly arrested and imprisoned, the legendary unit faces disbandment. When they are finally freed, Colonel Halil Ibrahim and Captain Kağan Bozok must rebuild the team and return to the frontlines, carrying out high-stakes missions that test their courage, brotherhood, and love for their country across 5 seasons and 109 episodes.
cast: Berk Oktay, Alican Albayrak, Uğur Biçer, Bahadır Vatanoğlu, Doğan Bayraktar, Ali Tarık Fındık, Yıldız Bozok, Murat Serezli

title: Dancing with the Stars Israel
overview: The Israeli version of the popular British TV show Strictly Come Dancing. The show features local celebrities partnered with professional ballroom dancers, competing to be the most successful dancers in the contest. Each week the couple that gains the least votes from the show's judges and spectators is eliminated. Viewers vote for their favorites, in order to save them from elimination, via phone, SMS and online. Over 350,000 votes were cast on the show's 3rd season finale.
cast: Lucy Ayoub, Eli Mizrahi, Anna Aronov, David Dvir, Lea Yanai

title: Cennet Mahallesi
cast: Melek Baykal, Çağla Şıkel, Alişan, Zeki Alasya, Şeyla Halis, Ümit Yesin, Aylin Kabasakal, Zihni Göktay

title: The Bride
overview: The continuation of the lineage of a deep-rooted family depends on Cihan Develioğlu's unborn son, but Cihan cannot have a child due to his wife. Mukadder plays a cruel game for her son Cihan to have a son. Mukadder goes to Cihan's wife, Beyza, with a dangerous proposal and asks her to divorce her son. Named after her sharp looks, Hançer faces an offer that will turn her life upside down. Mukadder, who takes advantage of the fact that Hançer is poor and has a family that will not cause trouble, offers her the opportunity to marry her son and give birth to a male child. Hançer, who dreams of finding the love of her life and having a happy marriage, first rejects this offer. However, she is forced to accept the offer when she learns that her brother has a deadly illness. Cihan and Hançer, who have entered an irreversible path, will no longer be the same. Will these two young people, drawn into a big game and lost in a dark maze, fall under the spell of love contrary to the planned games?
cast: Talya Çelebi, Cenay Türksever, Sidal Damar, Betigül Ceylan, Kutay Şahin, Can Tarakçı, Ceren Yüksekkaya, Çisel Kuşkan

title: Karadayi
overview: The Kara family's life is turned upside down because of a wrongful accusation that results in the imprisonment of Nazif Kara for a murder he didn't commit.
cast: Kenan İmirzalıoğlu, Bergüzar Korel, Çetin Tekindor, Yurdaer Okur, Erkan Avcı, Melike İpek Yalova, Rıza Kocaoğlu, Ulaş Tuna Astepe

title: Broken Pieces
overview: A story about difficult choices, tough decisions, entwined lives and parents torn between their children and their hearts. Gulseren comes from a poor background, while Cihans wife Dilara is wealthy. They both gave birth in the same hospital on the same day 15 years ago but a mistake was made that would change their destinies. Having similar surnames, an absent-minded nurse mixed the babies and nobody noticed. As the truth about the children is revealed, fundamental problems arise between the two families because of their totally different lifestyles and economic statuses. However these events bring Cihan and Gulseren closer together. They have inexplicable feelings towards each other that they cannot run away from or ignore.
genres: Action & Adventure, Comedy, Drama, Family, Crime
cast: Erkan Petekkaya, Ebru Özkan, Nurgül Yeşilçay, Barış Falay, Alina Boz, Mine Tugay, Leyla Tanlar, Civan Canova

title: Come What May
overview: Alize, who lost her mother the day she was born, is a beautiful but very selfish young girl who was spoiled and raised by her father. Alize, who goes crazy when she learns that her father is getting remarried, decides to fake a marriage with a man he would never allow her to marry in order to take revenge. However, things may not go as planned.
cast: Enes Koçak, Eylül Tumbar, Hakan Yılmaz, Selen Soyder, Hivda Zizan Alp, Yonca Şahinbaş, Berat Yenilmez, Sait Genay

title: Last Frame
overview: After being suspended from duty and only able to work under the supervision of a psychotherapist, Homicide Squad Commissioner Sare Baykal faces the most complicated murder of her career, while her path crosses with fashion photographer Atlas Demiroğlu, who came to the police station because of a film full of mysterious images. The investigation, which begins with a woman's body found in an amusement park, soon opens the doors to a multi-layered puzzle that extends to both Sare and Atlas' pasts. As each new clue draws Sare and Atlas into a dark labyrinth, the boundaries between reality, fantasy, loyalty and betrayal become increasingly blurred.
cast: Burcu Özberk, Uraz Kaygılaroğlu, Yiğit Özşener, Nizam Namidar, Cahit Gök, Nasmina Tüten, Muhammet Kulu, Onur Erdi

title: Circle
overview: A special team of four is established by the Istanbul Police Department. The team has all kinds of different talents. These four people, who have experienced many things in their past, start working together to solve grisly murders.
cast: Barış Bağcı, Pelin Akil, Anıl Altan

title: Scorpion
overview: Perihan, her daughter Berna, and their family live together comfortably. Ferda, Perihan's famous daughter, runs a beloved hair salon. Ferda blames her troubles on her mother's abandonment. Berna is married with three children. Ferda has an affair with Berna's husband, causing a tragic event. Duru, Perihan's granddaughter studying in the US, arrives for the funeral. Ferda surprises the family by calling Perihan "mother," revealing a secret. This leads to a war between mother and daughter, affecting their neighbors Ipek and Aras.
cast: Demet Akbağ, Evrim Alasya, Bekir Aksoy, Beril Pozam, Aslı Melisa Uzun, Yusuf Çim, Suzan Aksoy, Demet Gül

title: A Mother's Love
overview: Zeynep is an innocent and beautiful girl who's very fond of her mother. One day, her mother Cemre dies in an accident. No one can bring themselves to tell Zeynep that her mother is dead. Zeynep was born with a heart condition and she is not strong enough to bear the news. When she was dying, Zeynep's mother is slandered so that Murat, who used to love her madly, now hates her deeply. Now that Asude, her grandmother has the perfect conditions to make Murat marry Melek, the woman she'd like to see as her daughter-in-law. The night that Zeynep finds out about her mother's death, she also finds out that Murat is to marry Melek and she has an attack. When Zeynep's heart is about to stop, a miracle happens. Zeynep comes across Nazli, who looks exactly like her mother.
cast: Erol Gedik, Selin Sezgin, Gece Işık Demirel, Cansın Mısırlı, Serdar Yıldırım, Dilara Büyükbayraktar, Faruk Pakiş, Kayra Zabcı

title: Tozkoparan: İskender
overview: If Iskender, a 12-year-old archer with extraordinary abilities, does not return from 2020 to the time he lived, the course of history will change.
cast: Çağan Efe Ak, Tuana Naz Tiryaki, Yagiz Kilinc, Leya Kırşan, Deniz Erayvaz, Gökhan Atalay, Sera Tokdemir, Burak Alkaş

title: HaAlufa
cast: Adi Himelbloy, Michal Gavrielov, Ofer Shechter, Yehuda Levi

title: Land of Beautiful Love
overview: The heir of a wealthy Cappadocian family, Arhan Alaca, falls in love with the lively Izmir doctor Deniz Dervişoğlu.
